title: Optimistic Todo List
description:
Build a **Todo List** that performs **optimistic UI updates** — changes appear instantly in the UI, then roll back if the async operation fails.

### Requirements

- Render a list of todo items from `initialTodos` prop
- Provide an input + **Add** button to add new todos
- Each todo has a **Toggle** button (complete/incomplete) and a **Delete** button
- **All mutations (add, toggle, delete)** must use the provided `api` prop (simulated async operations)
- When a mutation is triggered, **immediately update the UI** (optimistic)
- If the API call **rejects**, **revert** the UI to its previous state and show an error toast
- The error toast should appear with `data-testid="error-toast"` and auto-dismiss after 3 seconds
- While an API call is pending, show a subtle loading indicator on that item (class `todo__item--pending`)

### Test IDs

| Element | data-testid |
|---------|-------------|
| Todo input | `todo-input` |
| Add button | `todo-add` |
| Todo item at index N | `todo-item-{index}` |
| Toggle button at index N | `todo-toggle-{index}` |
| Delete button at index N | `todo-delete-{index}` |
| Error toast | `error-toast` |
constraints:
- Use React functional components with hooks
- All mutations must go through the `api` prop — do not modify state without calling the API
- The optimistic update must be visually instant (no waiting for the promise)
hints: Save a snapshot of the current state before performing the optimistic update.
Use .catch() on the API promise to restore the snapshot and show the error toast.
Track which item IDs have pending operations with a Set in state.
For the add operation, generate a temporary ID optimistically, then replace it with the server response.
